records.ety: [L. extollere; ex out + tollere to lift, take up, or raise: cf. OF. extoller. See Tollerate, and cf. Flate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Extolled(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Extolling.]

defs: To place on high; to lift up; to elevate.
quotes: Who extolled you in the half-crown boxes, Where you might sit and muster all the beauties.

defs: To elevate by praise; to eulogize; to praise; to magnify; as, to extol virtue; to extol an act or a person.
quotes: Wherein have I so deserved of you, That you extol me thus?
